source: User requested web research for open-source graph visualization tools, then approved proceeding with implementation.
summary: Research-backed graph visualization selection for Control Room

diff_table.current_state: The Control Room Graph view uses a hand-rolled SVG grid renderer.
diff_table.desired_state: The Control Room Graph view uses Cytoscape.js for open-source interactive graph rendering.
diff_table.rationale: Research found Cytoscape.js is MIT licensed, browser compatible, supports built-in pan/zoom/select/events/styles/layouts, and fits the current vanilla local server without React.
diff_table.risk: Dependency and browser asset serving complexity.
alternatives: Sigma.js + Graphology for very large WebGL graphs.
vis-network for easy physics/clustering but weaker long-term fit.
React Flow for node-editor UI but requires React.
AntV G6 for broad graph engine features but larger complexity.
Raw D3/custom SVG continuation with more bespoke maintenance.

accepted_decisions.summary: Use Cytoscape.js as the first Control Room graph visualization engine.
Keep the Control Room local-first by serving the Cytoscape browser asset from the installed npm dependency rather than a CDN.
Keep Sigma.js + Graphology as a future option if graph scale exceeds Cytoscape.js comfort limits.
Use Dagre or ELK later for specialized directed architecture layout if Cytoscape built-in layouts prove insufficient.

assumptions: Current CodeWiki graph scale is small/medium enough for Cytoscape.js canvas rendering.
Avoiding a React migration matters more than React Flow's node-editor strengths.
non_goals: No graph editing UI in this task.
